capabilities.evidence: rcl_bridge.c:122-130 crcl_publisher_create rejects any type absent from crcl_marshal_registry.c, so route (a) (typed/serialized via rmw) cannot open a publisher; on that miss RclClient.createRawWriterPublisher falls back to route (b) — the CDDSBridge raw-CDR writer below rmw (sibling participant on the context domain, DDS topic/type via SwiftROS2Wire, USER_DATA typehash). Served outside rcl's entity graph (a documented divergence) but interoperable with any ROS 2 subscriber by topic + DDS-type name. Proven in-process by crcl-nonbundled-loopback (route-b .rcl publish -> .dds wire subscribe, same domain) and by RclNonbundledPublishIntegrationTests (LAN). Unblocks Conduit AudioData + CompressedPointCloud2 zenoh-rmw variant caveat: route-b is CycloneDDS-only and unreachable via rmw_zenoh, so the zenoh variant throws unsupportedFeature on a registry miss instead of silently publishing onto an invisible DDS domain (#155 fail-loud gate); bundling (#154/#158) is the mitigation for every shipped type.

latency.value: n/a — route-b raw-CDR = pure-Swift DDS path; perf = DDS baseline (M5)

capabilities.evidence: RclClient.createSubscription falls back to a CDDSBridge raw-CDR reader on a registry miss (mirror of the publish route-b): a sibling participant on the context domain, DDS topic/type via SwiftROS2Wire + USER_DATA typehash; received below rmw (outside rcl's wait-set, a documented divergence) but interoperable with any ROS 2 publisher by topic + DDS-type name. Proven in-process by crcl-nonbundled-sub-loopback (.dds wire publish -> .rcl route-b subscribe, same domain) and by RclNonbundledSubscribeIntegrationTests (LAN). Pairs with publish.serialized.non_bundled for full non-bundled pub/sub. zenoh-rmw variant caveat: route-b is CycloneDDS-only and unreachable via rmw_zenoh, so the zenoh variant throws unsupportedFeature on a registry miss instead of silently publishing onto an invisible DDS domain (#155 fail-loud gate); bundling (#154/#158) is the mitigation for every shipped type.

capabilities.evidence: MZ1: .zenoh(locator:) now routes to RclTransportSession in the zenoh-rmw variant via makeDefaultSession #elseif SWIFT_ROS2_RCL_RMW_ZENOH (zenoh-pico carved out). The router locator is injected into rmw_zenoh_cpp through RclClient.makeZenohSessionConfigJSON5 + applyZenohSessionEnv (ZENOH_SESSION_CONFIG_URI + ZENOH_ROUTER_CHECK_ATTEMPTS, a save/restore mirror of the DDS CYCLONEDDS_URI path), threaded through the createContext seam and RclTransportSession.open. RclZenohSessionEnvTests covers config shape, env apply/restore, and open() locator forwarding; the api-stability gate is green (createContext is a package requirement, public surface unchanged). MZ3 verified: end-to-end vs a live rmw_zenohd router (Docker, jazzy 0.2.9) — latency/soak/correctness recorded on this row; resource recorded in the MZ4 iPhone batch (PASS, documented binary-size divergence — see docs/verification-runbook.md). Build-time variant origin: M9 #128. visionOS caveat: the zenoh variant ships no xros/xrsimulator slices (zenoh-c dep pnet_sys lacks visionOS support) — visionOS stays on the wire path or .dds.

correctness.value: MZ3: live field-level ros2 topic echo agreement vs real Jazzy host for Imu/PointCloud2(120KB)/CompressedImage(64KB); RIHS01 type hash accepted by the rmw_zenoh graph; crcl-loopback + crcl-svc-loopback (AddTwoInts+params) + crcl-action-loopback (Fibonacci+cancel) green on the zenoh variant. Byte parity: crcl-golden 11/11 (M9); PointCloud2 rmw_serialize byte divergence on the fastrtps typesupport is semantically benign (live echo decodes correctly) — tracked as a documented divergence. environment: Docker rmw_zenohd router on localhost (ros_jazzy_zenoh, jazzy 0.2.9), LAN host down; native-LAN rerun queued | MZ5 Linux (ROS 2 Jazzy, system install; no xcframework): .zenoh -> rcl+rmw_zenoh_cpp (runtime rmw via RMW_IMPLEMENTATION); talker->listener 10/10 on /chatter via live rmw_zenohd (tcp/127.0.0.1:7447); timestamping fix (#160) present so no AdvancedPublisher SIGABRT

latency.value: MZ3 zenoh-rcl via local rmw_zenohd: publish-call p99 imu 113us / image64k 138us / cloud120k 233us (SLO 1ms); e2e roundtrip p99 622/483/643us @100Hz (SLO 2ms); roundtrip-lan via Docker relay 3.97/5.39/5.94ms p99 (relay+bridge dominated). Wire-zenoh comparison: publish-call p99 417/3988/4747us — rcl ~20x faster on large payloads. environment: Docker rmw_zenohd router on localhost (ros_jazzy_zenoh, jazzy 0.2.9), LAN host down; native-LAN rerun queued

resource.value: MZ4 iPhone 15 Pro, two signed Release builds on the merged #160 fix (rcl+rmw_zenoh+zenoh-c vs pure-Swift zenoh-pico baseline). Binary-size (arm64 Release exec): 24.22 MiB vs 11.76 MiB = delta +12.46 MiB -- DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE, exceeds the DDS-comparison <=2 MiB gate: structural, the zenoh-pico(C)->zenoh-c(Rust) transport swap (zenohc.rcgu.o alone = 17.76 MiB in librclros.a), the two zenoh libs cannot co-link so the delta is irreducible, not rcl-layer overhead. Runtime under camera+LiDAR load (xctrace Activity Monitor, per-process, 300s): rcl-zenoh running ALL 12 sensors = CPU mean 18.4% (max 20.8), phys-footprint 89.6 MiB, thermal Nominal; pure-Swift baseline running ONLY camera+LiDAR = CPU 72.8% (max 77.4), footprint 317.7 MiB, thermal Serious (camera degraded 24->17Hz under thermal throttle). rcl ~4x cheaper CPU and ~3.5x lower memory while running MORE sensors, staying Nominal. Capability: the pure-Swift baseline starves camera+LiDAR to 0Hz under full concurrent sensor load; rcl-zenoh sustains the full set -- the +12.5 MiB binary buys dramatically better runtime + capability. rcl Release launched clean on device, no SIGABRT (#160 timestamping fix holds). Battery not measured (USB-charging tether; non-discriminating). environment: LAN rmw_zenohd on youtalk-desktop.local 192.168.1.106, Jazzy, ROS_DOMAIN_ID=123.

soak.value: MZ3 zenoh-rcl 2x8h @100Hz --expect-echo: cloud120k rss_slope_b_per_min=-9129 fd_growth=0 tput_degradation_pct=0.0 published=2880000 received=2879863 verdict=healthy; image64k rss_slope_b_per_min=-19085 fd_growth=0 tput_degradation_pct=0.0 published=2880000 received=2879896 verdict=healthy. Fault run recorded separately. environment: Docker rmw_zenohd router on localhost (ros_jazzy_zenoh, jazzy 0.2.9), LAN host down; native-LAN rerun queued Fault run (1200s cloud120k, --inject-malformed, docker-restart of the router at t=300): verdict=healthy, publisher survived (100 msgs/s throughout), recv dipped 100->25/s and fully recovered within ~30s of the router returning (rmw_zenoh auto-reconnect), fd_growth=0, received 118741/120000 (98.9%; loss window = router downtime).

capabilities.evidence: pure-Swift wire codecs speak Humble/Jazzy/Kilted/Rolling (ROS2Distro); the RCL backend bundles the Jazzy stack only (CRos2Jazzy*). A non-Jazzy distro on an RCL-backed session is a documented limitation.

capabilities.evidence: pure-Swift wire ships + is CI-tested on Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 x86_64+aarch64. MZ5 landed the Linux RCL backend via a system ROS 2 install (ament prefix, not an xcframework): .zenoh -> rcl+rmw_zenoh_cpp and .dds/.rcl -> rcl+rmw_cyclonedds_cpp, with the rmw selected per-process at runtime via RMW_IMPLEMENTATION. Verified on Ubuntu 24.04 / ROS 2 Jazzy: crcl-loopback 20/20 in-process over cyclonedds; talker->listener 10/10 over a live rmw_zenohd router; SwiftROS2RCLTests green in the ci-rcl Linux leg. One process serves .zenoh OR .dds (the rmw is process-global) — switching transports needs separate processes. Latency + correctness axes measured 2026-07-11 on youtalk-desktop (see axis cells) after the #164 routing fix — the earlier MZ5 verification exercised .rcl configs and crcl-loopback below the public API, which is how the .dds ROS2Context regression slipped through. rcl=partial: the transport layer is now verified end-to-end on both rmws; the full per-type/service/action Linux capability matrix is not yet individually run.

correctness.value: Ubuntu 24.04 / ROS 2 Jazzy live field-level agreement (branch 1226766cc): ros2 topic echo --once matches talker output over BOTH runtime rmws — .zenoh via rmw_zenohd (domain 87) and .dds via rmw_cyclonedds_cpp (domain 88, the public-API path unbroken by the #164 routing fix). SwiftROS2RCLTests 29/29 on the Linux host incl. CrossBackendBytesTests byte parity and the .ddsMulticast end-to-end smoke; /proc maps confirmed the zenoh leg loads librmw_zenoh_cpp/libzenohc (rcl data path, not wire zenoh-pico).

latency.value: MZ5 scaled W4 run (Ubuntu 24.04 / ROS 2 Jazzy, Core Ultra 9 285, debug, 100 Hz, branch 1226766cc): rcl-bench publish p99 21.2-171.1 us and roundtrip p99 246.2-378.2 us across imu/image64k/cloud120k on BOTH runtime rmws (rmw_zenoh_cpp via local rmw_zenohd tcp/7447, rmw_cyclonedds_cpp) — all 12 runs within the Apple W4 SLOs (publish 1 ms / roundtrip 2 ms), 2000/2000 received on every roundtrip.

capabilities.evidence: pure-Swift wire (zenoh-pico) ships on Windows x86_64 and Android arm64/x86_64; RCL there is deferred. Windows (MZ6 spike 2026-07-11): upstream rmw_zenoh_cpp has supported Windows since Dec 2024 (ros2/rmw_zenoh#312, CI-tested upstream), but no official Jazzy Windows binary ships it (kilted/rolling ros2.repos only; community RoboStack aside), swift-ros2's RCL layer is Jazzy-pinned, and Windows verification is CI-only — re-gate when an official Jazzy-compatible Windows rmw_zenoh binary path exists or the RCL layer supports Kilted. Android: full-rcl NDK cross-build unsolved.
